studyGuide.title: Introduction
studyGuide.content: A triangle is a simple closed shape bounded by exactly 3 straight sides. The sum of all its inside angles is always 180°.

studyGuide.title: Types of Triangles
studyGuide.content:
**Equilateral:** All 3 sides are equal.
**Isosceles:** Exactly 2 sides are equal.
**Scalene:** No sides are equal.

studyGuide.title: Similar vs. Congruent
studyGuide.content:
**Similar:** Look like the same shape but different sizes (like toy cars vs real cars).
**Congruent:** Exactly the same shape AND size (like twin copies).

studyGuide.title: Basic Proportionality Theorem (BPT)
studyGuide.content: If you draw a straight line inside the triangle parallel to the bottom side, it cuts the left and right sides in the exact same matching ratio.

studyGuide.title: The Pythagoras Theorem
studyGuide.content:
In a right-angled triangle (90° corner), the biggest side (Hypotenuse) squared equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides:
**Hypotenuse² = Base² + Height²**

studyGuide.title: Important Points
studyGuide.content:
• The Hypotenuse is always the longest side.
• Triangles are the strongest shape used in building bridges.
• AAA, SSS, and SAS are rules to prove two triangles are mathematically similar.
